title: mentha × nemorosa var. crispa
intro: Mentha × nemorosa var. crispa, also known as pineapple mint or crinkled mint, is a hybrid plant species of the mint family, characterized by its unique crinkled leaves and sweet pine-like fragrance. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its essential oils, including menthol and menthone. The medicinal properties of Mentha × nemorosa var. crispa have been found to provide various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing stress and anxiety, and acting as a natural breath freshener. The plant can be used in herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in culinary and cosmetic applications, where its fragrance and flavor can be appreciated.
